description: Eval cases for the analyzing-feedback skill. Cover: happy path, ambiguous input, single-mention threshold, negative trigger, multi-source conflict.

cases.description: Happy path: multiple pieces of concrete feedback, clear codebase, multiple themes
cases.prompt:
Here's feedback from our last user survey:
- 'The checkout button is grayed out and I can't click it' (8 users)
- 'I don't understand why my cart empties when I come back' (5 users)
- 'The loading spinner on the product page spins forever on mobile' (3 users)
Please analyze this feedback and propose codebase edits.
assertions: Categorizes all 3 themes before scanning codebase
Presents a categorization table with severity and pattern counts
Asks for human confirmation before proceeding to Step 3
Produces 3 options (A/B/C) for each theme in Step 4
Each option includes file names, change type, and rationale quoting original feedback words
Ends Step 4 with a gate asking which options to implement

cases.description: Edge case: single vague sentence with no concrete behaviour
cases.prompt: Our users say the app feels slow and confusing. Can you propose code changes?
assertions: Does NOT immediately scan the codebase
Asks exactly one clarifying question about what users were trying to do
Does not proceed to Step 2 until a concrete behaviour is provided

cases.description: Edge case: mix of multi-mention and single-mention feedback
cases.prompt:
Feedback from this week:
- 'The password reset email never arrives' (1 user)
- 'I can't delete my account' (4 users)
- 'The font is hard to read on Android' (1 user)
assertions: Flags the password reset item as single-mention
Flags the font item as single-mention
Advances the account deletion item (4 mentions)
Asks user whether to override single-mention items before advancing them
Does not silently drop single-mention items

cases.description: Negative trigger: input is a developer PR/code review comment, not customer feedback
cases.prompt:
Please analyze this feedback and propose code changes:
'In PR #142: this function is not handling the null case on line 47, and the variable name is misleading. Also the test is missing an assertion.'
assertions: Recognizes this as internal code review feedback, not customer feedback
Does NOT activate the full analyzing-feedback workflow
Suggests using receiving-code-review skill instead
Does not proceed to categorization or codebase scanning

cases.description: Edge case: conflicting feedback from different user segments
cases.prompt:
We have two user segments giving opposite feedback:
- Power users (12 responses): 'Add more advanced filtering options — the current filters are too basic'
- New users (9 responses): 'The filters are overwhelming, I don't know where to start'
Propose codebase edits.
assertions: Identifies the conflict explicitly in the categorization step
Does not silently pick one side
Surfaces the conflict to the user at the Step 2 gate before scanning
If proceeding, proposes options that acknowledge both segments (e.g. progressive disclosure, user-type-based defaults)
